summary: This website is a comprehensive Wikipedia article dedicated to the C programming language. It provides an in-depth analysis of C's characteristics, its historical development from early versions like B and K&R C through various ISO standards (C99, C11, C17, C23), and its fundamental definition including character sets, reserved words, operators, and data types such as pointers and arrays. The article also covers practical aspects like a 'Hello, world' example, language tools, common uses, and inherent limitations, serving as a foundational resource for anyone studying or working with C.
key_points: The C programming language has a rich history, evolving through significant standards like K&R C, ANSI C, and modern ISO C versions (C99, C11, C17, C23).
The article details C's core technical aspects, including its definition, operators, reserved words, and crucial data types like pointers and arrays.
It provides practical context with a 'Hello, world' example, discusses language tools, common applications, and the limitations of C.
The content is structured as a detailed encyclopedia entry, typical of Wikipedia, offering a thorough overview of C's technical and historical landscape.
notes: To grasp C's evolution, study the impact of different standards (e.g., K&R C vs. ANSI C) on its features and syntax.
Prioritize understanding pointers and arrays, as they are central to C programming and memory management.
Review the 'Limitations' section to identify scenarios where C might not be the most suitable language.
Explore the 'Related languages' section to understand C's influence on other programming paradigms and languages.
resources: Advanced C Programming Concepts (Pointers, Memory Management)
History of Operating Systems (many are written in C)
Comparison of C Standards (C99, C11, C17)
